key_points: Threat: HIGH. Qatar has been targeted by Iranian missile strikes against Al Udeid Air Base and other military infrastructure. Airspace closure reflects continued risk.
Trend: STABLE. Missile attacks remain possible as long as Iranian retaliatory operations continue against US military assets.
Air Travel: DOH (Hamad International Airport): OPEN but flights currently canceled. Relief flights may continue to operate from alternative hubs such as Muscat or Riyadh.
Ground Travel: Primary exit routes involve land crossings into Saudi Arabia. These crossings remain open but are increasingly congested and could close rapidly if the conflict expands.

situation_assessment.assessment: HIGH. Qatar has been targeted by Iranian missile strikes against Al Udeid Air Base and other military infrastructure. Airspace closure reflects continued risk.
STABLE. Missile attacks remain possible as long as Iranian retaliatory operations continue against US military assets.
Presence of the largest US military base in the Middle East (Al Udeid), Iranian retaliation against coalition partners, regional escalation.
Continued interceptions of missiles and drones targeting US military infrastructure. Airspace remains closed.
Continued threat of missile strikes against US bases and associated infrastructure. Airspace likely to remain restricted.
Qatar likely remains a strategic target as long as coalition forces operate from Al Udeid. Aviation recovery unlikely until missile activity decreases significantly.
DOH (Hamad International Airport): OPEN but flights currently canceled. Relief flights may continue to operate from alternative hubs such as Muscat or Riyadh.
Primary exit routes involve land crossings into Saudi Arabia. These crossings remain open but are increasingly congested and could close rapidly if the conflict expands.

airports.name: Hamad International Airport
airports.notes: OPEN (facilities) but commercial flights currently canceled; limited relief/humanitarian movements possible.

borders.name: Abu Samra/Salwa (Saudi Arabia)
borders.notes: Primary land exit route into Saudi Arabia; congestion increasing per operator assessment.

domestic_unrest.notes: No significant unrest reported; population compliance with government guidance.

energy_infra.notes: LNG production disruption/force majeure risk materially impacts Qatar and global energy markets.
